architecture.summary: The system trains Sparse AutoEncoders (SAEs) on LLM layer activations using transfer learning. An SAE trained on one layer is fine-tuned on adjacent layers, leveraging shared representations across the LLM's depth to accelerate convergence. Information flow: raw layer activations → pre-trained SAE (source layer) → fine-tuned SAE (target layer); the pre-trained weights are warm-start initialization for downstream layers.

biblio.title: Accelerating Sparse Autoencoder Training via Layer-Wise Transfer Learning in Large Language Models

claims.statement: fine-tuning SAEs using pre-trained models from nearby layers not only maintains but often improves the quality of learned representations
transfer learning significantly accelerates convergence of SAE training
strategic reuse of pre-trained SAEs is a promising approach particularly in settings where computational resources are constrained
shared representations are found across adjacent layers of LLMs
